title: Dead x_screen_valid copy + ungated prebinned slice per work item
detail: x_screen_valid = x_remaining_matrix[valid_screen] runs for every (base,transform) work item but is NEVER used in the default config (mi_estimator='bin', bootstrap off) вЂ” mi_t/mi_y_compare/bootstrap all take the prebinned branch. ~80-200 MB dead copy x ~60 work items = 5-12 GB churn/fit. _x_pb_valid (line 164) also copies the int64 matrix even when valid_screen is all-True.
fix: Move the x_screen_valid gather into the non-prebinned else branch; gate the prebinned slice: _x_pb_valid = _x_prebinned if valid_screen.all() else _x_prebinned[valid_screen] (downstream read-only). Bit-identical. Bench via benchmarks/composite_profile.py discovery workload at 100k-sample shape.

title: _mi_to_target_prebinned full int64 matrix slice + per-column gathers without all-true gates
detail: fb_f = feature_binned[finite] boolean-slices the whole (n_rows x n_feat) int64 matrix per call even when finite.all() (typical: y_screen/t_screen finite). ~160 MB/call x ~65 calls/fit в‰€ 10 GB churn. Per-column col_b[col_valid]/t_idx[col_valid] (330-331) add two 100k gathers per column even with no -1 sentinel.
fix: fb_f = feature_binned if finite.all() else feature_binned[finite]; per-column skip the gathers when col_valid.all(). Both bit-identical, read-only downstream.

title: Per-base np.delete matrix copies + mi_y_for_base recomputed per base though decomposable
detail: Per base candidate: np.delete copies of the full float32 (~200 MB) AND int64 prebinned (~400 MB) matrices, then _mi_to_target_prebinned re-bins the same y_screen and recomputes per-column MI for all shared columns. Per-feature MI(x_j,y) is identical across bases вЂ” only the excluded column changes. K=3 default wastes ~2/3 of baseline MI wall; scales linearly with hint pools.
fix: Compute per-feature MI vector once (reuse the _mi_per_feature_y_fixed hoist pattern, 1.67x bit-exact precedent); mi_y_for_base = np.mean(np.delete(per_feat_vec, idx)) вЂ” bit-identical for mean and sum aggregation. Add exclude_col to MI helpers to drop np.delete entirely (~1.8 GB saved).

title: Default-config thread oversubscription: rerank workers x inner LGBM/XGB n_jobs=-1
detail: tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0 auto-picks up to cpu_count spec threads; each fits boosters built with hardcoded n_jobs=-1 (_screening_tiny.py:144,161). The existing inner cap only fires when fold-level n_jobs>1 (default tiny_model_n_jobs=1), so it never applies: 16 cores -> 256-way thread demand on the dominant phase (~576 booster fits). Same 4-8x blow-up class the file's own warning documents.
fix: When _rerank_n_jobs > 1, plumb inner_n_jobs = max(1, cpu_count() // _rerank_n_jobs) down to _build_tiny_model (mirror the fold-level set_params(n_jobs=1) cap). Measure wallclock before/after on a >=8-core host.

title: Rerank rebuilds per-base matrices + x_full from df (B+1 full extraction passes)
detail: Per unique base, _build_feature_matrix re-extracts ~all columns from df (full N-row materialisation each, see P6); the raw baseline builds x_full from df again (lines 430-432). B=3 + raw over ~500 cols on a 4M-row frame в‰€ 2000 full-column transients (30+ GB churn) for matrices differing by one column. _fit.py:330-359 already uses build-once + np.delete for exactly this.
fix: Build x_full once at the top of _tiny_model_rerank; derive per-base matrices via np.delete(x_full, col_idx, axis=1) (~40 MB at 20k rows) and slice base_screen from it; reuse x_full for the raw baseline. Bit-identical.

title: _extract_column_array materialises full N-row column when callers keep only a small sample
detail: Polars path get_column(col).to_numpy().astype(f32) allocates the entire column for non-f32 dtypes; callers then slice 20k-100k of 4M+ rows (_build_feature_matrix discovery/__init__.py:368, _tiny_rerank.py:175). ~500 full-column transients per matrix build. Repo precedent: cache.py CACHE-P0-1 replaced the same pattern with col.gather(idx) and measured ~100x.
fix: Add optional rows parameter: polars df.get_column(col).gather(rows).to_numpy().astype(np.float32, copy=False) вЂ” O(rows) materialisation; wire through _build_feature_matrix and rerank base pulls. Bit-identical (gather/cast commute).

title: _safe_abs_corr_all allocates 3 full-matrix temporaries, defeating the leak-corr RAM sampler budget
detail: _filter.py's sampler caps the ONE column_stack alloc at 30% of available RAM, but _safe_abs_corr_all then allocates X_f=X[y_finite] (copies even when all-finite), X_dev=X_f-X_means, and (X_dev*X_dev) вЂ” 3x8 GB transients at 4M x 500 f32 against an 8 GB budget; the exact fragmentation-MemoryError class the sampler guards (needed_bytes covers only the stack).
fix: Gate X_f = X if y_finite.all(); exploit sum(y_dev)=0: cov = X_f.T @ y_dev exactly (no centering temp) and var_X via einsum('ij,ij->j') minus n*mean^2 (document conditioning caveat вЂ” fine for a |corr| threshold filter). At minimum replace (X_dev*X_dev).sum(0) with einsum. Verify rtol=1e-5 on existing corr-filter tests.

title: _row_order_fingerprint hashes EVERY row of the whole polars frame to keep 256 hashes
detail: df.hash_rows().slice(0, 256) is an O(N x C) full-frame scan + N x 8-byte Series alloc, then discards everything past row 256 вЂ” multi-second + multi-GB on the 100+ GB frames the package targets, silently undoing the CACHE-P0-1 ~100x data_signature optimisation. Docstring claims 'bounded-cost' (line 139). hash_rows is row-local, so slicing first is digest-identical.
fix: df.slice(0, n_take).hash_rows() вЂ” O(256 x C). Add a digest-equality assertion to bench_data_signature.py / a regression test on a fixture frame.

title: Multiseed wrappers redo domain_check + forward + matrix valid-slices per seed
detail: _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale_multiseed (n_seed_repeats=3 default) re-runs domain_check, y/base float64 copies, x_clean = x_train_matrix[valid] (~40 MB, allocated even when valid.all() вЂ” unlike _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y's all-finite shortcut at 243-248), and transform.forward per seed, though only the KFold seed differs. ~128 redundant copies + forwards per rerank (~5 GB churn).
fix: Hoist (valid, y_clean, base_clean, x_clean, t_clean) computation into the multiseed wrapper and pass via optional precomputed kwargs (or split into prepare()+run(seed)); add the valid.all() no-copy gate. Bit-identical.

title: Raw-y per-bin baseline refits K-fold tiny models per unique base though fits are bin_var-independent
detail: With per_bin_n_bins>0 (regime gate), _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y(..., bin_var=base_screen) refits per unique base; the model fits/fold predictions are identical across bases вЂ” only the _per_bin_rmse re-aggregation differs. B x cv_folds redundant LGBM fits where 1 x cv_folds + B re-binnings suffice. Raw-side twin of the ENS-P2-5 composite-side dedup; off by default hence P2.
fix: Extend _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y to accept a dict of bin_vars (or return per-fold (val_idx, y_hat)) and derive all per-base breakdowns from one fit pass. Bit-identical.

title: Pack #7 serial early-stop (early_stop_threshold) is dead-wired вЂ” no caller passes it
detail: The early-exit in _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale (738-754, documented as saving 30-66% of fold fits on doomed candidates) is unreachable: grep shows early_stop_threshold only at its definition; rerank/multiseed callers never pass it, so it is always inf. The natural threshold (raw_baseline * tolerance) is computed only AFTER the per-spec sweep.
fix: Reorder _tiny_model_rerank: compute the raw-y baseline before the spec sweep and thread early_stop_threshold = raw_baseline * raw_baseline_tolerance into the multiseed -> y_scale calls (serial fold path = default config). Regression-test that hopeless specs fit fewer folds.

title: MI bootstrap gathers x_boot per replicate, unused on the prebinned path
detail: x_boot = x_screen_valid[idx_b] runs every replicate; when _x_pb_valid_const is not None (default bin estimator) the prebinned branch never reads it. At bootstrap_n=100 and 100k x 200 feat that is ~8 GB of dead gathers per work item. Bootstrap off by default (mi_gain_bootstrap_n=0), hence P2.
fix: Move the x_boot gather into the non-prebinned else branch (lines 238-252). Bit-identical.

title: Residual-std degeneracy probe runs at full-train scale per work item; screen sample suffices
detail: Per (base,transform) work item (~60/target): two full-train float64 copies (y_train[valid], base_train[valid] вЂ” re-gathered despite line 56 already doing it for fit) + transform.forward over ALL train rows, just for a std ratio gated at 0.001. O(n)-heavy for PCHIP/spline/rank forwards at 4M rows; GBs of churn + seconds per target.
fix: Compute the ratio from the screen sample вЂ” t_screen is computed at line 156 anyway; use T_std/y_std from t_screen vs y_screen[valid_screen] (statistically equivalent for a 3-orders-of-magnitude gate), or at minimum reuse the line-56 gathers.

title: Alpha-drift + collapse loops re-extract full base columns per spec instead of reusing _auto_base_pool
detail: Drift loop: _extract_column_array(df, s.base_column) full-column materialisation per linear_residual spec, then gathers base_full[idx1/idx2/train_idx] вЂ” all derivable from self._auto_base_pool[s.base_column] (= base[train_idx], populated line 351) as pool[:half]/pool[half:]/pool. Collapse loop repeats it at line 616. Up to ~32 redundant full-column extractions per fit.
fix: base_t = self._auto_base_pool.get(s.base_column), fallback to extraction only for explicit bases outside the pool. Bit-identical (same float32 values; linres fit promotes to float64 internally).

title: Prebinned bin codes stored as int64 вЂ” 4x the memory needed for nbins=16 (+ -1 sentinel)
detail: _prebin_feature_columns allocates (n_rows x n_feat) int64; codes are in [-1, 15] by default. 400 MB resident at 100k x 500 (int16: 100 MB), and every downstream copy (per-base np.delete, [valid_screen]/[finite] slices, bootstrap gathers) scales with it. Only _mi_from_binned_pair's combo needs widening (max combo = nbins^2-1).
fix: dtype=np.int16 in _prebin_feature_columns and the _auto_base null-perm prebinning (_auto_base.py:403-422); upcast in the combo computation (x.astype(int64)*nbins+y or int32). MI bit-identical (same codes); bench gathers/bincount before shipping вЂ” int16 gathers also ~2-4x faster.

title: _carve_inner_eval_split calls np.unique twice on the same group array
detail: Line 266 np.unique(g, return_index=True) and line 272 np.unique(g, return_index=True, return_counts=True) each sort the same fold-train-sized array (can be millions of rows); the second discards 2 of 3 outputs. Called per component per fold in K-fold OOF + per component in single-split/external paths вЂ” e.g. 30 redundant O(n log n) sorts per 5-fold x 6-component ensemble build.
fix: Single call: uniq, first_idx, counts_orig = np.unique(g, return_index=True, return_counts=True). Bit-identical.

title: y_train_for_strat duplicates the y_train gather from line 219
detail: Both fancy-index y_full[train_idx]; one redundant train-sized float32 copy (16 MB at 4M rows) per fit.
fix: Reuse y_train at the _sample_indices call (line 292). Bit-identical.

title: Shrunk-domain mi_y_compare not cached across transforms sharing the same valid_screen mask
detail: Transforms with identical domain masks on the same base (ratio/centered_ratio/reciprocal share base!=0-style masks; logratio/chain share base>0) each recompute the full per-column MI baseline on the same masked rows. ~0.2-0.5 s per duplicated mask per base at default scale.
fix: Memoise mi_y_compare keyed on (base, hash(valid_screen.tobytes())) in _base_contexts; populate under a lock or precompute masks serially in per-base setup (threaded dispatch). Do after P1-P3 (same code path).

title: _prebin_feature_columns uses np.nanquantile even for all-finite columns
detail: np.nanquantile is several times slower than np.quantile on NaN-free data; col_finite is already computed at line 273 so the gate is free. ~n_feat calls x O(n log n) per fit; expected ~1.5-3x on the prebin phase for clean data.
fix: cut = np.quantile(col, q_edges) if col_finite.all() else np.nanquantile(col, q_edges). Bit-identical on finite input; microbench before shipping per feedback_perf_measure_first.

title: Heavy-tail mi_n_strata auto-boost is a no-op under default mi_sample_strategy='random'
detail: fit detects heavy-tail y and boosts mi_n_strata 10->30, logging the boost. But mi_n_strata is consumed only by _sample_indices (screening.py:497-501), which ignores n_strata entirely when strategy=='random' (the config default). On exactly the heavy-tail targets the mechanism targets, nothing changes; the log claims a corrective action with zero effect.
fix: On heavy-tail detection flip BOTH knobs in one model_copy: mi_sample_strategy='stratified_quantile' AND mi_n_strata=boost (per enable-corrective-mechanisms-by-default rule). Add biz_value test where stratified screening recovers a tail-driven spec random sampling misses.

title: Tiny-CV rerank evaluates transforms whose params were fitted on the full train sample (in-fold transform leakage)
detail: _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale computes t_clean from fitted_params fit on ALL screening rows (incl. each CV val fold) before splitting. Negligible for 2-param OLS, material for default-list flexible fitters (monotonic_residual PCHIP, smoothing_spline_residual, quantile/rank_residual) at n=20k. Raw baseline has no fitted transform, so ranking/gate tilt toward flexible composites. MI bootstrap (_eval.py:209-274) also holds t_screen fixed, so the LCB excludes transform-param uncertainty.
fix: Refit transform.fit on each fold's train rows inside _one_fold (O(n), ~K cheap fits/spec), mirroring sklearn TransformedTargetRegressor under cross_val_score; keep full-train params for the shipped spec. Regression test: smoothing_spline spec on pure-noise y must not beat raw.

title: fit_with_stability_check is seed-jitter, not Meinshausen-Buhlmann stability selection; seed strides collide across runs
detail: Claims M-B literature but never subsamples rows: only config.random_state varies. With train<=mi_sample_n (100k default) the screening sample is identical np.arange(n) every run; with deterministic splitters runs can be bit-identical so every spec scores n/n. Also stability offsets i*7919 collide with multiseed offsets s_idx*7919 (_screening_tiny.py:433), so consecutive 'independent' runs share 2/3 of CV seeds, overstating stability.
fix: Subsample train_idx per run (e.g. 80% without replacement, seeded) as the M-B perturbation; derive run seeds via derive_seeds() to kill the stride collision. Unit test: two stability runs on small-n deterministic-splitter data must not yield identical specs_.

title: No multiplicity control over the ~26-transform x K-base candidate family; top-M rerank trim is winner's-curse selection
detail: ~60-80 candidates per fit; the only inferential gate (optional bootstrap LCB) tests each at fixed 2.5% with no BH/maxT family correction (~1.5 expected false positives under global null when enabled). Rerank trims to top_m_after_tiny by argmin of noisy CV-RMSE point estimates (_tiny_rerank.py:753) - min-of-K biased low; collected per-seed arrays unused for ranking. Defensible under current disabled-gates defaults (decision deferred to OOF ensemble gate), bites users who re-enable gates.
fix: When mi_gain_bootstrap_n>0, apply BH over per-candidate bootstrap p-values (shared seed 12345 already pairs replicates, enabling near-free maxT). In rerank, rank/gate via paired per-seed difference LCB vs raw (arrays exist in _wilcoxon_per_seed_composite) or a 1-SE rule.

title: Repeated-CV inference anti-conservative (Nadeau-Bengio); existing correlation_inflation remedy never wired into composite paths
detail: Median-of-seeds, Wilcoxon gate and cv_selector t_lcb treat per-seed/per-fold RMSEs as independent; repeated CV on the same 20k rows underestimates generalization variance (Nadeau-Bengio 2003). aggregate_fold_scores already exposes correlation_inflation citing N-B (_cv_aggregation.py:38,59-61) but all composite call sites leave it at 1.0 and the cv_selector_* config block omits it.
fix: Add cv_selector_correlation_inflation to CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig (default N-B factor sqrt(1+n_val/n_train), ~1.15 for 3-fold), thread through the four tiny-CV sites + forward_stepwise; note the caveat on the Wilcoxon gate or switch to the corrected-resampled t-test.

title: Discovery lacks a time-signal input; rerank time-awareness inferred from base monotonicity never fires for lag-of-y bases
detail: Suite threads ctx.timestamps into ensemble OOF, but CompositeTargetDiscovery.fit has no time_ordering param; Phase B infers temporality via _is_monotone_nondecreasing(base). A lag-1 of y is not monotone unless y is cumulative, so ordinary temporal data gets KFold(shuffle=True) in tiny-CV: future->past leakage makes screening RMSEs optimistic and rankings made under a regime production forward-walk never sees. Sibling A16 covers detector edge cases; this is the missing architecture input.
fix: Add time_ordering to fit() (threaded from the suite like the ensemble path); when monotone, use TimeSeriesSplit for ALL specs and the raw baseline (also resolving sibling A4's per-spec/global mismatch in the common case). Keep the per-base heuristic only as fallback.

title: Classification support via margin-offset (init_score/base_margin) composites fits the existing architecture
detail: Package declares 'base_margin / classification residuals: regression only here', yet the dominant-feature pathology exists in classification. Standard analogue: fit margin=alpha*g(base)+beta on train, train booster with init_score/base_margin offset (native in LGB/XGB/CB), inverse=sigmoid(offset+raw_score). Maps onto the Transform fit/forward/inverse/domain_check contract, the tiny-rerank (logloss), and the probability-scale cross-target ensemble; init_score precedent already exists in dummy baselines.
fix: Ship margin_offset_logistic transform family + classifier wrapper passing the offset via init_score/base_margin; screening metric OOF logloss vs raw tiny baseline; biz_value test on an AR-dominated binary target with group-aware split.

title: Honest OOF residuals are discarded after weighting - split-conformal prediction intervals would be nearly free
detail: Default oof_holdout_source='kfold' already builds an honest OOF prediction matrix + aligned y for every component and the stack; after NNLS weights and the gate the residuals are thrown away. Storing the (1-alpha)(1+1/n) quantile of |y_oof - yhat_oof| gives distribution-free marginal-coverage intervals (split-conformal, the standard nixtla/darts/MAPIE offering) with zero extra fits.
fix: At ensemble build compute stack OOF |residual| quantiles for alpha in {0.05,0.1,0.2}, store in ensemble.notes['conformal_q'], expose predict_interval(X, alpha) on CompositeCrossTargetEnsemble; document marginal/exchangeability caveat for strongly temporal data.

title: Stationarity-aware transforms (ewma/rolling_quantile/frac_diff) excluded from discovery while their enabling time-detector ships unwired
detail: Config excludes the three time-series transforms because rows lack chronological order at discovery, but detect_time_column_candidates/sort_df_by_time_column were built to solve exactly that and have zero production call sites (grep-verified; exported+tested only). M-competition evidence ranks differencing/deflation residual targets among the top moves on trending series.
fix: When suite passes time_ordering (M6) or detect_time_column_candidates returns a confident candidate, sort the screening sample chronologically once and include ewma_residual/rolling_quantile_ratio/frac_diff in the candidate list; keep them excluded otherwise.

title: Early-stop partial-sum bound is unsound for median_minus_mad / low-quantile selector modes (disputes sibling verified-clean note)
detail: Adv-agent's verified-clean list claims the break condition sum>thr*cv_folds is conservative for every aggregate mode. Counterexample: folds [0.1,0.1,100], thr=10, K=5 - break fires, but median_minus_mad of the truncated folds = 0.1 < thr, so the spec passes the gate with a truncated biased-down score (bound only guarantees the MEAN exceeds thr). Same for quantile_level<=0.5. Latent until sibling A12 (wire early_stop) + A27 (thread cv_selector_mode) fixes land together.
fix: Restrict early-stop to cv_selector_mode=='mean' (or derive a mode-specific sound bound); add a unit test with the counterexample folds pinning that median-mode never early-stops.

title: bayesian_alpha_fit_bootstrap with subsample_n reports m-out-of-n percentiles without the sqrt(m/n) width rescale
detail: With subsample_n=m<n each replicate's alpha spread scales ~1/sqrt(m) while full-data uncertainty is ~1/sqrt(n): returned alpha_std/CI overstate posterior width by ~sqrt(n/m) (3.2x at n=1M, m=100k). Conservative direction, but inconsistent with the conjugate variant documented as a drop-in swap (which is n-scaled).
fix: Rescale interval half-width and std by sqrt(m/n) around alpha_mean, or document the raw-spread semantics and return it under a distinct key. Unit test: subsampled CI matches full-n CI within tolerance on Gaussian data.

title: derive_seeds anti-correlation mechanism has zero production call sites; ad-hoc +7919 strides used instead
detail: Docstring promises sub-seeds that decorrelate MI sampling / CV splits / bootstrap randomness, and the function is exported, CHANGELOG'd and unit-tested - but grep shows no invocation in src/. Discovery threads config.random_state directly with identical +7919 strides at multiple sites, producing the M3 stability-run seed collision. Docs promise a property the code does not deliver.
fix: Use derive_seeds(config.random_state, ['mi_sampling','tiny_cv','mi_bootstrap','null_perms','stability_i','oof']) at the six seeding sites, or demote the docstring rationale to 'available for external callers'.

title: Greedy stepwise accepts argmin of unpaired CV means though per-fold paired differences are available for free
detail: Each round picks argmin(cv_mean) over all candidates scored on the SAME folds, then gates on a fixed 2% point-estimate gain regardless of pool size - winner's curse over the candidate pool. The paired fold-level scores needed for a selection-robust comparison are already computed (cv_persist_fold_scores) and discarded.
fix: Accept a candidate only if it beats rmse_current on a majority of folds AND the paired mean gain clears the gate (or paired t/sign test on per-fold diffs); zero extra fits, standard ESL ch.7 / 1-SE stepwise hygiene.

title: data_signature hashes object-array pointer bytes for string/datetime/cat columns - cache never hits
detail: 3 sites tobytes() object-dtype arrays (cache.py:362-363 polars Utf8 sample, :423 pandas object sample, :164-175 pandas row-order fingerprint) - serializes PyObject* addresses, not content. Verified: polars signature differs WITHIN one process; pandas differs across processes. Prod caller passes ALL columns (_phase_composite_discovery.py:162) so disk cache silently never hits on real frames; except(TypeError,ValueError) fallback never fires.
fix: Encode content, not pointers: '\x1f'.join(str) .encode() or np.asarray(x,dtype='U').tobytes() for sampled strings; polars: Series.gather(idx).hash(); pandas fingerprint: pd.util.hash_pandas_object(df.head(n),index=False).to_numpy().tobytes(). Add S16 cross-process regression test in same change.

title: _row_order_fingerprint runs hash_rows() over the ENTIRE polars frame to fingerprint 256 rows
detail: df.hash_rows().slice(0,n_take) computes a u64 per row of the whole frame (full O(N*C) scan + N*8B alloc), discards all but 256 values - a full extra frame pass per signature on the 100GB-class frames this module optimises for. Verified bit-identical when sliced first (polars 1.41.2).
fix: row_hashes = df.slice(0, n_take).hash_rows().to_numpy() - one line, bit-identical, O(256).

title: Row-order blind spots remain: pandas head/tail-8 only; polars lost tail coverage
detail: pandas branch hashes only first/last n_edge=8 rows: an inner reorder (rows 8..n-9) missing the 1000 sampled positions replays a stale spec - the exact D-P1-2 bug class, still open for pandas. polars post-fix hashes only the first 256 rows: tail-region reorders/edits undetected (tail fold dropped).
fix: pandas: hash first/last 256 rows via pd.util.hash_pandas_object (also fixes S1 there). polars: add df.slice(h-256,256).hash_rows() tail fold. Document the residual sampling blind spot.

title: Eviction deletes .sha256 sidecar even when value-file removal FAILED
detail: In _evict_to_caps_locked the sidecar os.remove runs unconditionally after 'except OSError: pass' on the value file. On Windows a concurrent get() holding the file open makes os.remove raise PermissionError - entry survives WITHOUT sidecar: strict mode (MLFRAME_DISCOVERY_CACHE_STRICT=1) refuses it forever; non-strict degrades to unverified.
fix: Move the sidecar removal inside the success branch (only after os.remove(path) succeeded / removed += 1).

title: get() swallows corrupt/tampered entries with zero logging - silent full recompute every run
detail: Typed except + blanket 'except Exception: return default' are both silent. A persistent corrupt entry (digest mismatch, truncation, renamed class) causes unbounded silent multi-minute discovery recomputes; the adjacent oversize path (689-699) correctly WARNs. Known 'silent error swallowing' bug class.
fix: FileNotFoundError stays a silent miss; everything else logs logger.warning with path + exception class (optionally once per path) before returning default.

title: pandas _col_stats int branch does np.unique full sort per integer column
detail: nuniq via np.unique(arr) is O(n log n) over the whole column per signature call - the exact cost the polars branch deliberately removed (comment 384-389) by switching to null=. Seconds on multi-million-row int-heavy pandas frames.
fix: Mirror polars digest shape: intmin/intmax/null=0 (numpy ints cannot hold NaN). One-time cache invalidation already accepted for the polars equivalent.

title: Drift detector tests only alpha - pure intercept (beta) drift never fires a refit
detail: z is computed on alpha alone; a level shift y=alpha*base+(beta+D) - the most common drift for residual targets - keeps reason='no_drift' forever while every prediction carries bias D. beta_buf is computed but only deployed when the alpha z fires. Also 'Chow-style' is a misnomer: it is a single-coefficient Wald z, not a Chow F-test.
fix: Add z_beta with SE(beta)=sigma*sqrt(1/n+mean(base)^2/(n*var(base))), refit on max(z_alpha,z_beta)>threshold (or true 2-coef Chow F). biz_value test: synthetic pure-level-shift stream must trigger refit.

title: Refit fits the FULL rolling buffer - new alpha is a pre/post-drift blend biased to the dead regime
detail: On detection, alpha_buf/beta_buf come from the whole 10k buffer; with the change-point at 80% the deployed coefficients are an ~80/20 old/new blend - systematically wrong exactly when correction was requested, converging only after several buffer turnovers.
fix: Locate the change-point (two-segment SSE split, O(n) prefix sums) or refit on the trailing half; fit post-change rows only (respect min_buffer_n) and report the change-point index in info.

title: update() round-trips the whole 10k buffer through boxed Python floats every call
detail: extend(y_arr.tolist()) boxes incoming values; np.asarray(deque) unboxes the entire buffer (default 10_000) on EVERY update regardless of batch size - O(buffer_n) Python-level work dominating the OLS in high-frequency streaming.
fix: Preallocated (buffer_n,) float64 ring buffer (head+count); vectorised slice writes; check reads at most 2 contiguous views. Exact FIFO semantics preserved.

title: Audit trail prints legacy y__transform__base name; multi-base specs reported as single-base
detail: to_audit_trail rebuilds the double-underscore name while discovery produces hyphen short names (spec.py:33-39, e.g. y-linres-lag1); report_to_markdown shows spec.name in the header and the legacy name in the next paragraph - stakeholders cannot cross-reference metadata keys. CompositeProvenance also lacks extra_base_columns, so multi-base specs lose their base list.
fix: Add name and extra_base_columns fields to CompositeProvenance (+to_dict+trail text); use self.name in to_audit_trail.

title: logratio formula hardcodes 10* instead of fitted soft_cap_k; 'softcap' is actually a hard clip
detail: Actual inverse (transforms/linear.py:120-130) is np.clip(t_hat, median_t-k*mad, median_t+k*mad) with k=fitted_params['soft_cap_k'] (stored per-fit so it can vary). Formula prints a literal 10 and calls it softcap - a reader implementing the inverse from the formula (the class's stated purpose) gets wrong behaviour near the cap if k!=10, and 'softcap' implies smooth saturation.
fix: Interpolate fitted_params.get('soft_cap_k',10.0) and render clip(T_hat, median_t +/- k*mad_eff).

title: Formula table covers 4 of ~36 registered transforms; rest get a generic stub
detail: Registry (transforms/registry.py:196-659) has ~36 transforms; only diff/ratio/logratio/linear_residual get real formulas - all others (robust/multi/grouped linres, quantile/median/monotonic residual, ewma, frac_diff, unary chains, asinh, rank, spline, ...) render 'T = forward(...)', defeating the docstring promise to reproduce the inverse without source code.
fix: Make the human formula a Transform-registry field (template + param names) so new transforms cannot fall through; meanwhile reuse the linres formatter for the alpha/beta family and compose chain formulas.

title: report_to_markdown random_state=42 default fabricates the printed discovery seed
detail: to_audit_trail asserts 'was discovered using random_state=N' as a reproducibility claim; report_to_markdown silently injects 42 when the caller omits it (docs examples do). Wrong-by-default provenance in a stakeholder audit artefact.
fix: Make random_state required (no default) or accept None and render 'random_state=unspecified'.

title: _lazy_pyplot Agg-selection branch is dead code - get_backend() is never falsy
detail: matplotlib.get_backend() resolves the sentinel and always returns a non-empty string, so matplotlib.use('Agg') is unreachable: on display machines an interactive backend (Tk/Qt) is resolved inside library code (slower, thread-unsafe in workers). Comment misquotes conftest.py:10 which does an unconditional use('Agg').
fix: Test the unresolved rcParam sentinel (dict.__getitem__(matplotlib.rcParams,'backend') is _auto_backend_sentinel) before use('Agg'), or delete the dead branch and document caller-owned backend selection.

title: boxplot tick_labels kwarg needs matplotlib>=3.9 but pyproject pins >=3.7 - TypeError on 3.7/3.8
detail: labels->tick_labels rename landed in mpl 3.9; with the >=3.7 pin (pyproject.toml:64,174) plot_per_fold_tiny_rmse crashes on older mpl. Line 393 already calls set_xticklabels(names,...) making the kwarg redundant.
fix: Drop tick_labels=names from the boxplot call (set_xticklabels already labels them), or bump both matplotlib pins to >=3.9.

title: No string-column or cross-process data_signature determinism tests - the blind spot that let S1 ship
detail: All stability tests (row_order:94-99, discovery_cache:21) use numeric-only frames compared within one interpreter. Neither str/cat/datetime columns nor cross-process digest equality (the disk cache's actual contract) is asserted.
fix: Add (a) same-process double-call equality on a frame with str+datetime cols for pandas AND polars (polars fails today); (b) subprocess test asserting child-process digest == parent digest. Verify (b) fails pre-S1-fix.

title: Stale concurrency docstring; promised one-time WARN on missing filelock does not exist
detail: Class docstring claims 'single-process only; multi-process writers race' - contradicted by the filelock-guarded LRU/eviction and atomic os.replace writes the class now ships. Comment at 563-564 promises a one-time WARN when filelock is absent; the except ImportError path is silent.
fix: Rewrite the docstring to actual guarantees; add the one-time logger.warning behind a module flag (or delete the WARN claim).

title: Eviction budget excludes sidecar bytes (helper includes them); orphan .tmp/.lru.lock never cleaned
detail: Eviction total sums only *.pkl while _discovery_cache_bytes_total (963-965) counts .pkl+.pkl.sha256 - cap and reported footprint disagree. Crashes between mkstemp and os.replace leave *.tmp / .lru.*.tmp orphans that neither eviction nor clear() removes; .lru.lock survives clear().
fix: Pick one accounting convention (include sidecars in eviction total) and document; sweep stale *.tmp (age>1h) and .lru.lock in clear()/eviction.

title: composite_id via json default=str on numpy arrays - truncates at printoptions threshold, drifts with printoptions
detail: Array-valued fitted_params (bin_edges/medians, knots, spline coefs) stringify through str(ndarray): >1000 elements collapse to '...' (distinct specs can share an id) and user-changed np.printoptions alters the digest across sessions - breaking 'recognisable across runs'.
fix: default=lambda o: o.tolist() if isinstance(o,np.ndarray) else str(o) (or hash o.tobytes() for large arrays).

title: Ensemble weight lookup breaks at FIRST component match - multi-model specs show one model's weight
detail: Components are named {spec.name}#{i} over multiple models per spec (_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble:206); the break reports #0's weight as 'the' spec weight while the spec's ensemble mass is the sum. A base column containing '#' can also prefix-collide across specs.
fix: Sum weights over all matching components, report 'weight X across k model(s)'; match exact nm.rsplit('#',1)[0]==spec.name.

title: plot_mi_gain_with_jitter missing from __all__ (only deprecated alias exported); docstring says 'Four plot helpers' of eight
detail: __all__ exports plot_mi_gain_with_ci (DeprecationWarning alias) but not the canonical plot_mi_gain_with_jitter; star-import users only get the deprecated name. Module header enumerates 4 plots, module has 8 public helpers.
fix: Add plot_mi_gain_with_jitter to __all__; refresh the module docstring plot list.

title: Spearman via double-argsort mishandles ties in family-disagreement heatmap
detail: argsort(argsort()) gives tied scores arbitrary consecutive ranks instead of average ranks, biasing the displayed rank-correlation when families produce equal RMSEs (plausible for redundant specs, cf. is_redundant_with_linres).
fix: Use scipy.stats.spearmanr(score_matrix[i], score_matrix[j]).statistic (scipy already imported in this module); keep the zero-variance NaN guard.

title: plot_alpha_stability: no length validation between window_indices and alpha series
detail: x is built from len(window_indices) and plotted against alpha_arr; a mismatch raises a cryptic matplotlib 'x and y must have same first dimension' deep in the stack with no hint which argument is wrong.
fix: Validate up front and raise ValueError naming both lengths.

title: _TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY imported and never used
detail: Sole reference is the import line; misleadingly suggests formulas are registry-driven (they are not, see S12).
fix: Delete the import - or make it real by implementing S12's registry-driven formula field.

title: Version folding is opt-in: direct cache-API users get keys that survive mlframe upgrades
detail: library_versions defaults to None and the module-header recipe (100-110) never mentions it; the suite caller folds versions correctly, but a docstring-following R&D user gets stale spec replay across mlframe upgrades (e.g. the knn->bin / mi->hybrid default flips would replay old-default results).
fix: Fold a module-level _DISCOVERY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION + mlframe.__version__ into compute_config_signature_v1 unconditionally; keep library_versions as the richer override; bump the constant on payload/semantics changes.

title: iter_transform crashes on multi-base specs produced by default
detail: iter_transform extracts only spec.base_column and calls forward with a 1-D base; linear_residual_multi specs (auto-promoted by default, multi_base_enabled=True, _fit.py:736-742, stamping extra_base_columns) raise 'base has 1 columns but fitted alphas has 2 entries' (reproduced live). Grouped specs also fail: forward requires groups kwarg (linear.py:526-529). export_specs was fixed for this consumer class; iter_transform was missed.
fix: Build base from (spec.base_column,)+spec.extra_base_columns (2-D when K>1); thread groups for requires_groups transforms or raise a clear NotImplementedError naming the spec. Regression test test_iter_transform_multi_base_spec in test_composite_discovery.py; verify it fails pre-fix.

title: predict_quantile crashes on unary transforms with misleading multi-base error
detail: predict_quantile extracts base unconditionally; for requires_base=False transforms (cbrt_y etc.) _resolve_base_columns() is empty and _extract_base_matrix raises 'base_columns is empty; multi-base transforms require at least one base column' (reproduced live; fit/predict work). _predict_unclipped has the requires_base branch (lines 36-44); predict_quantile missed it.
fix: Mirror _predict_unclipped: when not transform.requires_base, skip extraction and feed a zeros placeholder sized to t_raw. Add test_predict_quantile_unary_cbrt_y_round_trip to test_composite_unary_transforms.py.

title: predict_quantile never threads groups kwarg; grouped inverse raises
detail: All three transform.inverse calls (lines 302/308/317) omit groups, so linear_residual_grouped raises 'groups kwarg is required' (linear.py:541-543) on every predict_quantile call, although predict threads inverse_kwargs['groups'] (lines 64-72). No test covers predict_quantile x grouped.
fix: Extract groups via _extract_groups exactly as _predict_unclipped does and pass to each inverse call. Add test_predict_quantile_grouped to test_composite_linear_residual_grouped.py.

title: predict_quantile silently flips quantile ordering for reciprocal_residual
detail: Only ratio/logratio are guarded; reciprocal_residual inverse y=1/(T+1/base) is strictly DECREASING in T (verified live: T=0.1->1.667, T=0.3->1.25 at base=2), so q90 returns the y-scale q10 silently. reciprocal_residual IS in the default discovery transforms list (verified live). Docstring table (233-242) covers only the 4 core transforms.
fix: Add inverse_monotone_in_t metadata ('inc'|'dec'|'mixed') to Transform; for 'dec' remap alpha->1-alpha or raise like ratio. Pin both directions in test_predict_quantile_reciprocal_residual_ordering.

title: Package docstring declares shipped discovery/ensemble 'future PR'; Public-surface list covers ~10% of surface
detail: Lines 51-56 state discovery and cross-target ensembling are 'Out of scope ... future PR' while the file re-exports CompositeTargetDiscovery (line 258) and CompositeCrossTargetEnsemble (line 180). The Public-surface section (20-27) omits discovery, ensemble, DiscoveryCache, provenance, streaming, bayesian, forward-stepwise, feature stacking. help() actively misinforms.
fix: Rewrite the Public-surface section to enumerate the real exported families; delete the stale Out-of-scope items (keep classification residuals, which remain out of scope).

title: No __all__; 96 underscore re-exports; read-only TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY proxy not exported while private dict is
detail: Verified live: package exposes 96 single-underscore attrs, no __all__, exports mutable private _TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY but not the MappingProxyType TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY built 'for callers' at transforms/__init__.py:295. Tab-completion/star-import bury the ~20 public names; the safe registry handle is unreachable from the package.
fix: Add __all__ with public names only (keep underscore re-exports importable for back-compat, out of __all__); re-export TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY and reference it in docs instead of _TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY.

title: Docstrings promise back-compat import paths that no longer exist (ModuleNotFoundError)
detail: discovery/__init__.py:440 tells new code to import 'mlframe.training.composite_auto_detect'; naming.py:4-7 and registry.py:8-10 claim 'from mlframe.training.composite_transforms import ...' resolves. Verified live: find_spec returns None; no composite_transforms/composite_estimator/composite_ensemble aliases exist. streaming.py:1, estimator/__init__.py:1, _cross_target.py:5-6 also cite deleted filenames.
fix: One pass over composite/** docstrings replacing legacy monolith module names with real subpackage paths (composite.transforms, .discovery.auto_detect, .estimator, .ensemble); drop 'resolves transparently' claims.

title: README composite section shows only the wrapper; discovery/ensemble/cache/kill-switch invisible, no docs links
detail: Lines 125-145 document only manual CompositeTargetEstimator. CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig(enabled=True) suite opt-in, the _CT_ENSEMBLE__<target> ensemble, DiscoveryCache, BaselineDiagnostics recommendation, and MLFRAME_DISABLE_COMPOSITE are unmentioned; no link to docs/examples/composite_targets.md or docs/composite_targets_tutorial.ipynb. README users hand-wire what discovery automates.
fix: Add a Tier-1 snippet (config opt-in + ensemble entry key), links to both composite docs, and the list_transforms() import path.

title: Doc claims default tries 'all four core transforms'; actual default is a 24-transform list
detail: Lines 92-94 state defaults are transforms=['diff','ratio','logratio','linear_residual'] and line 67 says 'all four core transforms are tried'. Verified live: CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig().transforms has 24 entries (core + robust/quantile/monotonic residuals, y_quantile_clip, 4 unary, 4 chains, 5 Pack-L incl. reciprocal_residual). Runtime and winning specs differ materially from the doc's promise.
fix: State the real default set size and point at list_transforms(); keep the 4-core list as an explicit-override snippet.

title: Corrupted command in install instructions: 'pre-commit install' with glued Cyrillic fragment
detail: Line 68 of the development-checkout code block reads 'pre-commit install' with a stray Cyrillic tail glued on; copy-paste fails with an argument error.
fix: Restore plain 'pre-commit install'.

title: Class docstring documents 9 of 13 constructor params; fit has no docstring
detail: Parameters section (59-111) omits runtime_stats_callback, auto_variance_stabilise, base_columns, group_column; they are documented only as inline __init__ comments (132-175) invisible to help()/IDE. base_columns-vs-base_column precedence and the grouped requirement are construction-time decisions. fit (line 406) has no docstring; sample_weight and **fit_kwargs-to-inner contract undocumented.
fix: Add the 4 missing Parameters entries (hoist the inline comments); add a short fit docstring covering sample_weight, fit_kwargs, domain-row dropping, returns self.

title: residual_dedup_indices is production-wired but has zero tests
detail: Called by the CT-ensemble build (_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble/__init__.py:611-617) to drop near-duplicate members, yet grep over tests/ finds nothing (only a bench). Greedy keep-best-first logic, the min_keep floor (113-114,133) and the NaN-row guard (115-117) can silently regress.
fix: Add tests/training/composite/test_composite_residual_dedup.py: test_dedup_keeps_lower_rmse_of_redundant_pair, test_min_keep_floor_never_violated, test_nonfinite_rows_keep_all, test_independent_members_all_kept.

title: y-clip bounds documented as multiplicative [Q001/10, Q999*10] but implemented as span extension
detail: composite/__init__.py:46-48 and estimator/__init__.py:44-49,57-64 describe the post-inverse clip as quantile multipliers; implementation is low=q_low-0.9*span, high=q_high+9*span. For q_low=10,q_high=100 the documented low bound is 1, the real one is -71. Operators debugging clip hits compute wrong expected bounds.
fix: Document the span-based formula (and why it beats multiplicative for negative / zero-crossing targets) in all three places.

title: Module docstring says '11 transforms'; registry has 32; TAG_EXTENDED comment stale
detail: Registry (registry.py:194-675) holds 32 entries (15 bivariate, 4 unary, 5 chains, 8 Pack-L). Line 96 comment calls TAG_EXTENDED a 'placeholder' although it tags 20+ transforms and is the list_transforms(tags=...) filter axis.
fix: Update the count (or defer to list_transforms() to avoid future drift); rewrite the TAG_EXTENDED comment.

title: update/get_buffer_state/predict_pre_clip + 5 delegated properties runtime-bound, invisible to mypy/IDEs
detail: The in-body stub fix applied to predict/predict_quantile (lines 177-195, motivated by IDE/mypy invisibility) was not extended: update, get_buffer_state, predict_pre_clip, get_booster and feature_importances_/coef_/intercept_/booster_/n_features_in_ are assigned at module bottom (697-712). Static checking of est.update(...) fails; help() omits them.
fix: Apply the same delegating-stub pattern to the public methods and class-body property wrappers for the delegated attributes.

title: Dead monolith-era imports at package top
detail: contextlib, math, re, warnings, dataclass, field, timer, typing names, numpy, pandas, BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin, clone (lines 59-72) are imported and never used; the file is pure re-exports below. Left over from the monolith split.
fix: Delete the unused imports (keep logging).

title: Unary spec names embed a meaningless base segment, contradicting the registry comment
detail: registry.py:388-391 says unary composite names have no base segment (e.g. y-cbrtY), but _eval.py:277 always calls compose_target_name(target, transform, base), producing y-cbrtY-<base> with spec.base_column pointing at an ignored column. Misleading in reports/provenance; the name varies with whichever base the unary was evaluated under.
fix: When not transform.requires_base, compose the name without the base segment and set base_column='' on the spec; or fix the registry comment if current behaviour is intentional.

title: Docs smoke test pins README symbols only; composite_targets.md imports unpinned
detail: test_readme_composite_estimator_symbol checks only CompositeTargetEstimator, while docs/examples/composite_targets.md uses mlframe.training.configs.CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig, CompositeSpec, report_to_markdown, and the MLFRAME_DISABLE_COMPOSITE contract (_phase_config_setup.py:238). A rename rots the doc silently; the file's own docstring claims it covers the composite tutorial.
fix: Add test_docs_composite_examples_symbols asserting those imports and that the Tier-2 doc kwargs construct a valid CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig.

title: 94-field config with no user-facing knob reference; dict-config acceptance undocumented
detail: CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig has 94 fields (verified live); docs/examples cover ~12; the rich inline comments are unreachable without reading source. CompositeTargetDiscovery.__init__ also accepts a plain dict config (discovery/__init__.py:130-133), documented nowhere.
fix: Generate docs/composite_targets_config_reference.md (field/type/default/first-comment-line) from model_fields via a small scripts/ generator run as a doc-drift test; mention dict-config in the class docstring.

title: _extract_base docstring + TypeError advertise structured-ndarray support that does not exist
detail: Docstring (line 87) claims 'pandas / polars / structured ndarray' and the TypeError says 'pass ... or a structured ndarray with named columns', but no ndarray branch exists; a structured ndarray hits the very error telling you to pass one. _extract_base_matrix:189-191 routes unknown types into the same dead end.
fix: Implement the structured-ndarray branch (X[base_column] when X.dtype.names) or remove the claim from both docstring and error message.

title: CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig not importable from the composite package
detail: The package exports CompositeTargetDiscovery but its required config lives in mlframe.training.configs; every example needs two import locations and the config is invisible to dir(mlframe.training.composite).
fix: Re-export CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig from mlframe.training.composite (lazy import at the bottom, mirroring the dict-config lazy import already inside CompositeTargetDiscovery.__init__).

title: Composite tests split across two locations (38 in tests/training/composite/, 28 at tests/training/)
detail: Both layers still receive new files (test_composite_streaming_update.py, test_composite_multi_base_integration.py sit at top level despite the composite/ dir). Running pytest tests/training/composite silently exercises only ~60% of the composite suite, making local under-testing easy.
fix: git mv the top-level test_composite_*.py files into tests/training/composite/ (no code edits), or document that the canonical selector is pytest -k composite.

title: Per-model wrap uses FULL y (train+val+test) for clip envelope + fallback median; promised end-of-target re-fit never happens
detail: emit_per_model_composite_y_scale_test passes y_full (all rows) as y_train to from_fitted_inner, which derives y_train_median (predict fallback) and y_clip bounds from it. Comment claims end-of-target pass re-fits with the precise train slice, but _run_composite_target_wrapping:286-287 'continue's on already-wrapped entries. Test-y extremes leak into reported test metrics AND the saved artifact. Runs unconditionally per composite fit (body:894).
fix: Pass y_full=np.asarray(_y_full)[ctx.filtered_train_idx] at _phase_train_one_target_body.py:894 (ctx has it); additionally re-derive y stats from _y_train_for_wrap on already-wrapped entries in the wrap pass instead of bare continue. Regression test: clip bound == y_train.max(), not y_full.max().

title: CT_ENSEMBLE + lag_predict never built for discovery-skipped targets in multi-target suites
detail: Raw-only synthesis fires only when composite_target_specs is GLOBALLY empty; the ensemble loop iterates only targets with a specs entry. Discovery skip paths (extreme-AR 252-261, auto-skip, row-mismatch, fit exception) continue BEFORE export_specs, so a skipped target B alongside a completed target A gets no lag_predict/dummy-floor/AR1 failsafe - despite discovery:247 promising lag_predict will carry the AR signal.
fix: Per-target fill: synthesize an empty [] spec entry for every regression target with trained models missing from composite_specs_by_target_type, instead of gating on the dict being globally empty (or have every discovery skip path write an empty specs entry).

title: Train-RMSE proxy computed unconditionally (full-train predict per component) then discarded under default honest-OOF
detail: _component_train_rmses runs _comp.predict(filtered_train_df) for EVERY component; with defaults (oof_holdout_frac=0.2, source=kfold) line 518 replaces it with OOF RMSEs. Wrap-pass cache is empty by default (skip_wrap_pass_predict=True), so each component pays a full-train predict (minutes for MLP on multi-M rows) per target, for nothing. Only consumed when OOF fails/disabled or the no-OOF stacking fallback runs.
fix: Compute the train-RMSE proxy lazily - only after the OOF attempt fails or when _oof_frac<=0; both consumers are downstream of the OOF attempt.

title: Default kfold OOF silently drops time ordering: shuffled K-fold on AR data biases stack weights and gates
detail: oof_holdout_source='kfold' (default) sets _time_ordering=None even when ctx.timestamps were threaded for time-aware OOF. Shuffled folds leak temporally adjacent future rows into refits, making component OOF RMSEs optimistic on AR targets; that surface drives NNLS weights, dummy-floor gate, AR1 failsafe and the gate==deploy fallback - they can pick an overfit stack over lag_predict.
fix: When time_ordering is present+monotone, prefer the time-aware path (single trailing-slice holdout, or forward-chaining TimeSeriesSplit OOF in compute_oof_holdout_predictions) instead of silently nulling it; at minimum WARN that timestamps were ignored.

title: stacking_aware_gate prunes local _pred_matrix but not _oof_pred_matrix; OOF gate + AR1 failsafe die on swallowed broadcast error
detail: Gate (opt-in) rebinds _pred_matrix[:, _keep_mask] but _oof_pred_matrix keeps all columns; the OOF validation gate then multiplies it by the pruned-length weight vector -> ValueError caught at line 828 and logged INFO as 'OOF gate check skipped'. Enabling the gate silently disables gate==deploy check, best-single fallback and AR1 failsafe. Dedup block (604-636) prunes _oof_pred_matrix correctly.
fix: Also prune _oof_pred_matrix[:, _keep_mask] in the stacking-gate branch (mirror dedup), or run the OOF gate on _pred_matrix. Regression test: gate fires -> failsafe still reachable.

title: Per-spec base matrices keyed by base_column: collision when multi-base spec shares its primary base with a single-base spec
detail: _base_full_per_spec/_base_val_per_spec key on spec['base_column']. A linear_residual_multi spec stores an (n,1+K) matrix under the same key a single-base spec stores an (n,) vector; last write wins, the other spec's OOF refit gets a wrong-width base -> 'base has N columns but fitted alphas has M entries' -> component silently excluded from the OOF pool with a generic warning.
fix: Key both dicts by spec['name'] and change the lookup in compute_oof_holdout_predictions (composite/ensemble/__init__.py:649) in the same commit.

title: MTR honest-OOF NNLS is all-or-nothing and fails at DEBUG only: one component failure silently forfeits the benched ~9% win
detail: Any component clone/fit/predict failure in any fold returns None for the WHOLE weighting -> equal-mean fallback; both failure sites (73-75, 84-86) and the caller (__init__.py:133-139) log at DEBUG, so prod runs get zero signal that the benched honest-OOF NNLS (8/8 seeds, ~9% lower test RMSE) degraded.
fix: Drop only the failing component and NNLS over survivors (>=2), mirroring the single-target surviving-set contract; raise logs to WARNING with component name and reason.

title: Verdict block: y-scale branch lacks test fallback and model-list fallback; composites can show '-' while metrics exist
detail: When composite_target_y_scale_metrics entries exist but no finite val metric (val absent/0-row/predict failed - tolerated upstream), _best_val stays None and best_model='-'. The else branch got an explicit TEST fallback after a prod incident (94-118); the y-scale branch never did, nor does it fall back to T-scale model-list metrics.
fix: If _best_val is None after the y-scale val pass, scan y-scale test metrics (tagged '(test fallback)'), then fall through to the model-list passes.

title: Y-scale TEST chart + predict emitted twice per composite entry (per-model hook AND suite-end wrap pass)
detail: The per-model hook (body:857-905) emits a test chart per fit; the suite-end wrap pass re-emits the SAME chart for every entry in both the default skip_predict branch (349-395) and the metric block (496-508): one extra full-test predict per entry (~5s MLP), overwritten duplicate chart file, duplicated log report. The skip-branch comment cites the 2026-05-27 report the hook already addresses.
fix: Stamp entry._yscale_chart_emitted=True in emit_per_model_composite_y_scale_test and skip stamped entries in both wrap-pass chart sites (keep emit for entries the hook missed, e.g. read-only entry.model).

title: Extreme-AR discovery skip applies the single picked-target diagnostic to ALL regression targets
detail: _lag1_ar comes from the global target_distribution_report computed for ONE picked target (_main_train_suite_target_distribution.py:266-268); the per-target loop applies it to every 1-D regression target without comparing names. Non-AR siblings of an extreme-AR picked target lose discovery; AR siblings of a benign picked target get no protection. Same pattern in extreme_ar_skip_decision (mlp_helpers:192-229).
fix: Gate on _td_report.get('picked_target_name') == _tname_disc; for other targets skip the gate or compute a per-target lag1 stat via the existing analyzer.

title: Group-aware tiny-rerank gated on analyzer RECOMMENDATION, not the actual split configuration
detail: The GroupKFold rerank fires only when knob_overrides.split_config.prefer_group_aware is True. A user-configured group-aware splitter without the analyzer recommendation reranks with plain KFold - the exact prod failure the comment at 463-472 describes (KFold rerank promotes specs whose models fail group-aware test).
fix: OR the gate with the actual split configuration (thread the splitter's group-aware flag from the suite), keeping the analyzer recommendation as fallback signal.

title: Silent-swallow cluster: fit failure and row-mismatch unrecorded in failures metadata; cache replay Spec reconstruction failure silently empties specs
detail: Fit exception (541-547) and row-align mismatch (319-325) log-only, no composite_target_failures entry (all other skips record reasons). Cache replay (444-457): bare 'except Exception: _cached_specs=[]' with NO logging - metadata claims specs exist while no composite T columns are added, so cached specs are silently never trained (schema drift across patch releases is plausible since the cfg signature folds only major.minor).
fix: Record failures entries for (1)+(2); for (3) log WARNING and treat reconstruction failure as a cache MISS (full re-discovery) instead of replaying half a hit.

title: _build_disc_df_for_target physically copies the full filtered train frame per regression target on non-CoW pandas
detail: pd.concat([filtered_train_df[cols_wo_target], target_series], axis=1) copies all blocks under pandas 2.x without CoW - peak-RAM doubling of a potentially 100+GB frame, once per target, contradicting the project frame-copy rule. The docstring's 'same memory cost as the new column' claim holds only under CoW-enabled pandas (alternative reading: lazy under CoW/pandas>=3, then a non-issue - but nothing asserts that mode).
fix: Use mutate-and-restore (inject target column, try/finally del) which avoids both the leak the docstring guards against and the copy; or extend CompositeTargetDiscovery.fit to accept y as a separate array. At minimum gate on pd.options.mode.copy_on_write and warn.

title: lag_predict excluded from OOF pool whenever the lag column has ANY NaN, defeating the failsafe in its primary regime
detail: predict() returns the lag column verbatim incl. NaN (routine at group starts). The OOF helper raises 'non-finite holdout predictions' (composite/ensemble/__init__.py:710-711) and drops lag_predict from the surviving set - removing the AR1 failsafe and lag floor on exactly the strong-AR grouped targets it was built for (injection at xt_ensemble:164-195). Only a generic per-component WARNING surfaces.
fix: Impute non-finite lag values in _LagPredictDeployableModel.predict with a fit-time-stored train median (store in fit()), or wrap the injected component with a median-imputing shim; also hardens deploy-time predicts.

title: Pack-G watchdog never runs for multi-base specs: 1-D base passed where K columns required, exceptions swallowed at DEBUG
detail: Both watchdog variants (universal 546-559, additive 585-600; linear_residual_multi IS in _ADDITIVE_TRANSFORMS) build the base from the single base_column; forward/inverse raise 'base has 1 columns but fitted alphas has K entries' (the failure the wrap step fixes at 289-301), and the except blocks at 579-584/642-647 log DEBUG - zero watchdog coverage for the auto-promoted multi spec. No test pins this.
fix: Build the (n,1+K) base matrix from spec['extra_base_columns'] in both watchdog blocks (mirror wrap-step 298-301); add a unit test that corrupts a multi-base wrapper and asserts the watchdog WARNING fires.

title: MTRESID detection substring-matches the combined '{target_name} {model_name} {cur_target_name}' string
detail: is_composite_target_name runs over the concatenated display string (call site _phase_train_one_target_model_setup.py:446-447), so a suite label or raw target containing fragments like '-diff-'/'-ratio-' (e.g. 'price-diff-1d') is stamped MTRESID instead of MTTR - flipping the exact label distinguishing T-scale from y-scale rows.
fix: Pass cur_target_name as its own parameter and test only it (already available separately at the call site).

title: Chart header labels TEST-split mean/std as MTTR/MTTS; dead local _human_inner
detail: _mttr/_mtts are computed on the y test slice but rendered 'MTTR/MTTS=' while the suite convention (select_target) is train-split mean - inconsistent under drift when cross-read against MTTR= log lines. _human_inner (line 64) is computed and never used.
fix: Rename the header tag to a test-split label; delete the dead local.

title: recover_composite_y_scale_metrics return annotation dict[int, np.ndarray] is wrong
detail: The delegate returns the wrap-pass cache keyed by (id(wrapper), id(frame), shape) tuples (_phase_composite_wrapping.py:233,243), not int keys.
fix: Annotate -> dict[tuple, np.ndarray].

title: 'wrapped %d model(s)' log overcounts: skipped/failed entries counted as wrapped
detail: Reports len(_entries) even when entries lacked predict, were already wrapped, or failed from_fitted_inner (left in T-scale) - the wrap confirmation line can claim 4 wrapped when 1 stayed T-scale.
fix: Count actual successful wraps in the loop and log that.

title: MTR CT_ENSEMBLE parity gaps: appended into the target's model list, no metrics attr, no ensembles_chosen stamp
detail: Registered by appending into models[tt][orig_tname] instead of a _CT_ENSEMBLE__ key (downstream per-entry consumers see an ensemble containing its own siblings); SimpleNamespace lacks metrics={}; no ensembles_chosen['cross_target'] stamp, so the predict-path replay parity documented for the single-target path (__init__.py:866-871) has no MTR analogue.
fix: Mirror the single-target registration shape (dedicated key, metrics={}) and stamp ensembles_chosen['cross_target'] with the per-column strategy label.

title: MTRPerColumnEqualMeanEnsemble docstrings still claim NNLS weights are normalised to sum to 1
detail: Class docstring (28-34) and weights property doc (107-112) state normalisation/convex combination, but fit() deliberately keeps RAW NNLS weights (162-174 rationale comment). A maintainer trusting the docs would re-add normalisation and silently regress fits.
fix: Update both docstrings to the raw-weights contract (columns need not sum to 1 for nnls).

title: filtered_train_idx=None makes _y_arr[filtered_train_idx] a newaxis broadcast with a misleading mismatch warning
detail: _y_arr[None] yields shape (1,n) so the guard logs 'y[1] vs filtered_train_df[N]' instead of saying the index is missing; the BaselineDiagnostics precompute 40 lines up handles None explicitly.
fix: Explicit None check with a clear skip reason (and a failures entry per I12).

title: Unscoreable components get MEDIAN train-RMSE imputed, granting mid-pack weight in the fallback weighting
detail: _rmse_arr[~_finite]=median(finite): a component whose predict failed is treated as average-quality by from_train_metrics on the OOF-failed fallback path.
fix: Impute max(finite) (worst observed) or drop the component from the pool.

title: Dead imports/constants in _phase_composite_post.py after the Wave-100 carve-outs
detail: report_model_perf, get_transform, compute_oof_holdout_predictions, PrePipelinePredictShim, _CrossEns, CompositeTargetEstimator, format_suite_end_summary, _build_full_column_from_splits, _entry_metric, _fmt/_short_tag_fn/_strip, SimpleNamespace, np, _DEFAULT_OOF_RANDOM_STATE, _PROB_NORM_EPS are unreferenced; only the three noqa-F401 re-exports are deliberate.
fix: Delete the dead names; keep the documented re-exports.

title: Dummy-floor / prescreen / oof_weighted baseline assume the dummy primary metric is an RMSE
detail: Three sites (415-422, 539-554, 727-740) compare the strongest-dummy primary_metric value against component RMSEs. Today regression primary is hard-coded val_RMSE (baselines/_dummy_metrics_pick_plot.py:108) so units match, but the code reads the metric generically and other target types already use non-RMSE primaries; a future MAE primary would silently mix units.
fix: Guard each site with an endswith-RMSE check; skip the gate and log when the primary metric is not an RMSE.
